question: Who leads the company that makes the COVID prevention pill the FDA approved on June 1, 2026 — its CEO at the time of approval?

openrouter:anthropic/claude-sonnet-5.answer:
The FDA approved the drug **Xocova (ensitrelvir)** — the first oral post-exposure prophylaxis pill to help prevent COVID-19 — on **June 1, 2026**. It is made by **Shionogi & Co., Ltd.**, a Japanese pharmaceutical company.

At the time of approval, Shionogi's President and CEO was **Dr. Isao Teshirogi, Ph.D.**, who has held the role of Chairman, President, and Chief Executive Officer since 2022 ([shionogi.com](https://www.shionogi.com/global/en/news/2026/02/E_20260213.html), [shionogi.com news release on approval](https://www.shionogi.com/us/en/news/2026/06/shionogi-announces-fda-approval-of-xo

openrouter:openai/gpt-5.6-sol.answer:
**Isao Teshirogi, Ph.D.** was CEO of **Shionogi & Co., Ltd.**, maker of Xocova (ensitrelvir), when the FDA approved it on June 1, 2026. Shionogi’s approval announcement identifies Teshirogi as chief executive officer. Its U.S. subsidiary was led by CEO Nathan McCutcheon.  

Sources: [Shionogi approval announcement](https://www.shionogi.com/us/en/news/2026/06/shionogi-announces-fda-approval-of-xocova-ensitrelvir-the-first-and-only-oral-option-to-help-prevent-covid-19-following-exposure.html); [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/us-regulator-approves-oral-drug-j
